emotional_core: The disorienting moment when love quietly overrides your internal operating system — not with chaos, but with the precise, unwanted loss of the coordinates you relied on to navigate yourself.
visual_metaphor: A moth in slow orbit around a candle — not falling, not fleeing, just perpetually drawn — against a backdrop with no visible horizon: no ground, no sky, just warm amber light pressing against deep dark.
key_quotes: my heart keeps enjoying the hangover you created
No ground to stand on. No sky to cover my head with.
Some people just become part of your physics.

story_frames.visual: A cold cup of coffee on a desk at night — laptop open, code on screen, warm but solitary atmosphere, steam long gone
Close-up of a moth in slow motion orbiting a candle flame, wings catching gold at the edges, dark background, no other light source
story_frames.text: Like a moth to the flame.

story_frames.visual: Ocean waves breaking and retreating in slow motion, cool blue-grey, relentless and indifferent, foam pulling back then returning without hesitation
Frost forming across a window pane in time-lapse, cold geometry spreading inward, soft winter light filtering through the growing ice patterns
A rainbow arching over an empty road after rain, saturated colors against grey clouds, no people, just the arc against the grey
story_frames.text: Like a rainbow after the rain.

story_frames.visual: A figure standing at a cliff edge in dense fog — no visible ground below, no sky above, just grey suspension on all sides — shot from directly behind
Return to the candle from frame 2 — this time tight on the flame itself, steady and warm, burning nothing visible but burning all the same, slight flicker
story_frames.text: The heart wants what it wants.
